Web search results: M17.1 is for UNilateral primary osteoarthritis; M17.0 is for BILATERAL primary osteoarthritis. Task description says 'Gonarthrose bilatérale' (bilateral) but uses code M17.1 (unilateral). See https://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/M00-M99/M15-M19/M17-/M17.0 and https://www.icd10data.com/ICD10CM/Codes/M00-M99/M15-M19/M17-/M17.1

Task description - realistic dosing. Clinical trials range 1.95-4g/day, so 1g dose is standard analgesic dosing. Paracetamol is first-line OA treatment per clinical guidelines. See https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6509082/
Task description - realistic treatment. Physical therapy is evidence-based for knee OA; 10 sessions is reasonable for individualized therapy. See https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11037114/
Task description - French for 'Re-evaluation in 1 month', reasonable follow-up interval for OA management

privileged_info_summary: The add_consultation task is based on MedinTux 2.16.012, a real free healthcare management software available on SourceForge. The patient case 'DUBOIS Marie-Claire' is synthetic data created at runtime via SQL INSERT statements into the DrTuxTest database (tables IndexNomPrenom and fchpat). The clinical scenario is realistic: 63-year-old patient with bilateral knee osteoarthritis (gonarthrose bilatérale), presenting with chief complaint of knee pain, vital signs (BP 132/84, HR 76 bpm) within expected ranges for age, weight 73 kg. Treatment plan is evidence-based: paracetamol 1g three times daily (standard first-line OA analgesia), physical therapy 10 sessions (supported by clinical evidence), with 1-month re-evaluation interval. CRITICAL ERROR IDENTIFIED: The diagnosis code is incorrectly specified as M17.1 (unilateral primary osteoarthritis) when the clinical description states 'Gonarthrose bilatérale' (bilateral), which should use ICD-10 code M17.0 (bilateral primary osteoarthritis of knee).
